mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366166M as of 2006-09-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-09-01 is in restrictive territory
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which erodes corporate profit margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both series to move in the specified direction simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135613k as of 2006-09-01 indicate tight labor market
failure_condition: When both UNRATE < 4.7% and PAYEMS growth > 150k/month, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 2% year-over-year signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is always-on and does not require regime activation.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.7063 (2017=100) as of 2006-08-01 reflects industrial strength
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1600k units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates broader economic slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1665k units as of 2006-08-01 is near the threshold
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1600k, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals monetary policy is restrictive and real rates are positive, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both monetary tightness and inflation pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-09-01 indicate restrictive monetary stance
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL > 203.5, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter predicts S&P 500 returns with a 20-day lag. Strong GDP growth (> 2.5% annualized) signals robust economic fundamentals and produces positive returns; weak growth (< 1.5%) predicts negative returns. The mechanism is regime-conditional and operates differently in expansion versus contraction phases.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11388.1B (2017 dollars) as of 2006-04-01 reflects strong demand
failure_condition: In expansion regimes, when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and forward-looking earnings pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of 2006-09-01 is below threshold but trending upward
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 4.8%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.
